instructions.description: Instructions: Explains the Decision Table format — how sets of boxes are described with counts and amounts, examples with positive and negative values, and how clicking highlights a set yellow to determine final earnings
Instructions: Explains 'The Average Box' payment rule — computer pays the average (sum/100) of all boxes in the chosen set, with examples of positive ($8) and negative (-$4) average payoffs
Instructions (Final Part): Explains that participants choose between Set A and Set B across multiple versions (rows) of the decision table, with an example 10-row table

screens.description: MPL decision table for task G10 (Mirror treatment): Set A = 100 boxes all containing $X (decreasing from $25 to $1 across rows) vs Set B = 10 boxes containing $25 + 90 boxes containing $0. Participant selects preferred set for each of 25 rows.
